business.headline: Oracle's layoffs explained: Why the IT giant suddenly fired 30,000 employeesIndia appears to be among the worst-hit markets, with reports indicating that nearly 12,000 employees may have been laid off out of a workforce of around 30,000.

IndiGo shares jump 9%: Why are aviation stocks rising today?IndiGo shares climbed as much as 8.33% to Rs 4272.20, while SpiceJet rose over 4.4% to Rs 10.17 around 9:48 am, signalling continued buying interest in the sector.

Sensex surges 1,500 points: Why is the stock market rising today?Today's surge is being powered by hopes of easing tensions, cheaper oil and a bounce in oversold stocks, but investors will be watching if the rally has real legs.

Sensex, Nifty see sharp rally on hopes of Iran war de-escalation; IndiGo up 4%The S&P BSE Sensex rallied 1,868.41 points to 73,815.96, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 576.45 points to 22,907.85 as of 9:26 am.

Will rupee slide to 100 against the US dollar as Iran war drags on?Rupee has declined by around 10% over the past year and is among the worst-performing currencies in Asia this year. Analysts say that while authorities are trying to slow the fall, these steps may only provide short-term support.

Jet fuel price up by 8.5%; govt says partial hike to shield domestic travel costsATF prices have been revised upwards, but government intervention has capped the impact on domestic airlines, even as uncertainty persists for international fares.

India's tobacco trap: Can jobs and public health be balanced?Although India has committed internationally through the WHO Framework Convention on Tobacco Control (FCTC) to reduce tobacco-related harm, policy decisions sometimes contradict this.

Commercial LPG price hiked by Rs 195.5, mini cylinder rates up by Rs 51In Delhi, the price of a 19-kg commercial LPG cylinder has been increased by Rs 195.50, bringing the new retail cost to Rs 2,078.50.

Thinking of foreclosing your loan? Here's what it does to your credit scoreGot some extra cash and thinking of closing your loan early? It may sound like a smart move, but many borrowers still wonder — what does it do to your credit score?

Will flight ticket prices rise from April?Air travel may get a bit more expensive from April as fuel prices are expected to rise. With airlines facing higher operating costs, will passengers soon have to pay more for tickets?

8th Pay Commission questionnaire deadline today: Have you submitted your response?The clock is ticking for central government employees and stakeholders. With the 8th Pay Commission questionnaire deadline ending today, those who want to share their inputs need to act fast.

Willie Walsh named IndiGo CEO, succeeds Pieter Elbers who quit in MarchIndiGo brings in global aviation veteran Willie Walsh as CEO, replacing Pieter Elbers, as the airline pushes ahead with international expansion and prepares for a more competitive long-haul market strategy.

Your credit report is about to change every week: What borrowers must knowBy the middle of 2026, the era of the monthly credit update will be entirely behind us. The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is actively pushing the boundaries of credit reporting, shifting the mandate to ensure that consumer credit data is updated with Credit Information Companies (CICs) on a weekly basis further bettering the existing fortnightly frequency.

Why did Atanu Chakraborty step down from HDFC Bank? Key reasons explainedAtanu Chakraborty resigned as HDFC Bank chairman due to a growing mismatch between his views on ethics and the bank's approach to certain issues, including governance and conduct matters.

Floating vs fixed home loans in 2026: What works better in a volatile market?Borrowers with stable income growth, diversified savings, and the ability to prepay opportunistically may benefit from floating structures. The flexibility to reduce principal during bonus cycles or surplus phases often offsets interim rate volatility.

Final call for taxpayers: 5 easy ways to reduce your tax before March 31 endsWith just hours left before the financial year closes, tax planning becomes urgent. The good news is that some easy investment options can still help you reduce your tax if you act quickly. Let's have a look.

US gasoline crosses $4 a gallon amid Iran war, highest since 2022The US national average retail price of gasoline moved past the $4 mark on Monday. The last time prices were at this level was in August 2022, following Russia's invasion of Ukraine.
Are banks open or closed on Mahavir Jayanti 2026?If you are visiting a bank today for personal work, it is best to check in advance whether your branch is open and what services are available.

IRDAI to introduce simple health covers, rate insurers and hospitals: ReportIRDAI's new steps aim to improve transparency and accountability in the health insurance sector, while addressing long-standing issues such as claim delays, billing disputes, and low insurance penetration.

ITR forms for AY 2026–27 out: Who can file ITR-1 and when should you choose ITR-2?The income tax filing season is here again, and the first step is knowing which form to pick. While ITR-1 works for many, others may need ITR-2, but how do you decide which one applies to you?

entertainment.headline: Akshay Kumar visits Ujjain's Mahakaleshwar Temple ahead of Bhooth Bangla release Akshay Kumar visited the revered Shri Mahakaleshwar Temple in Ujjain to seek blessings for his upcoming film Bhooth Bangla and the well-being of his family and country.

Taylor Swift honours Hollywood legend Elizabeth Taylor in new music video Taylor Swift has released the heartfelt music video for Elizabeth Taylor, paying tribute to the iconic actor Elizabeth Taylor. The video is exclusively available on Spotify and Apple Music.

Pak politician Nabil Gabol reacts to portrayal of Dawood Ibrahim in Dhurandhar 2 Dhurandhar: The Revenge has sparked real-world chatter. Pakistani politician Nabil Gabol has reacted to a scene featuring Dawood Ibrahim in the film.

It wasn't meant to be: Zayed Khan reflects on Hrithik–Sussanne separation Zayed Khan opened up about Hrithik Roshan and Sussanne Khan's divorce, their focus on co-parenting, and finding happiness after separation.

Rhea Kapoor shares first pic of Sonam-Anand's son. Kareena, Karisma send love Sonam Kapoor and Anand Ahuja welcomed their second son on March 29. Her sister, Rhea Kapoor, shared the first picture of their baby boy.

Releasing on April 3, the film explores love, relationships, and the fallout of difficult choices.

Shweta Tripathi opens up on Mirzapur: The Movie, spills the beans on Season 4 Shweta Tripathi spoke about Mirzapur's impact on her career, her character Golu's evolution, and how Mirzapur: The Movie feels like a celebration of everything the show has built. In an exclusive conversation, she also spoke about Season 4 of Mirzapur.

Dhurandhar 2's Rs 1,500 crore shakes Bollywood: Can Toxic, Ramayana carry momentum? Dhurandhar: The Revenge has rewritten box office rules with its massive Rs 1,500 crore run, leaving the industry both thrilled and uncertain. As films like Ramayana, Maatrubhumi and Toxic line up, trade experts decode what this historic success means for Bollywood's next big phase.

Tovino Thomas said no to Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's Dragon. He explains why Tovino Thomas stepped away from Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's Dragon recently. At a recent event in Hyderabad, the actor explained the reasons behind his decision to let go of the project.

Dakota Johnson says handshake at audition cost her a role. Here's why Hollywood actor Dakota Johnson recently shared an experience from a callback audition. She recalled losing a role and being labelled pompous over a simple greeting.

Cape Fear teaser: Martin Scorsese's thriller gets a dark, modern series reboot The teaser of the Cape Fear series introduced Javier Bardem as the menacing Max Cady. Backed by Martin Scorsese and Steven Spielberg, the psychological thriller will premiere on June 5.

Allu Arjun 3.0: Is he betting big on fantasy and superhero films at the right time? After Pushpa, Allu Arjun's next lineup with Atlee, Lokesh Kanagaraj and Basil Joseph hints at a clear shift towards fantasy and superhero cinema.

Mark Ruffalo raises alarm over possible nuclear attack on Iran, calls it insanity Actor-director Mark Ruffalo has called for urgent global action to avert a nuclear disaster in Iran. Describing the situation as insanity, he criticised world leaders and warned that millions of innocent lives hang in the balance.

Dhurandhar 2 box office day 13: No slowing down, film nears Rs 1,500 crore globally Dhurandhar 2 continues its blockbuster run at the box office. It is about to surpass the Rs 1,500-crore mark globally in just 13 days. The film has already surpassed the lifetime collection of its predecessor and shows no signs of slowing down.

Malayalam director-producer Ranjith arrested on sexual assault charges Prominent Malayalam director-producer Ranjith Balakrishnan has been arrested on sexual assault charges filed by a female actor. He was taken into custody, based on a complaint filed with the Kochi police.

Supergirl trailer: Milly Alcock, Jason Momoa lead James Gunn's gritty superhero film The trailer of Supergirl: Woman of Tomorrow was released on March 31. Milly Alcock's Supergirl faces new dangers to protect her loyal dog, Krypto, from the alien villain Krem of the Yellow Hills.

Newlyweds Vijay, Rashmika all smiles at wedding with Anand Deverakonda. See pics Anand Deverakonda attended a wedding ceremony with Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna. The newlyweds, alongside Anand, were seen in traditional attire at the event.

Raja Shivaji teaser: Riteish Deshmukh is fierce as Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj The teaser of Raja Shivaji was released on March 31. Riteish Deshmukh plays Maratha warrior king Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in the upcoming period drama.

Mammootty, Mohanlal's Patriot release postponed, makers to share new date soon The much-awaited Malayalam espionage thriller Patriot, starring Mammootty and Mohanlal, has been postponed due to unforeseen circumstances. The new release date will be announced on April 1, 2026.

What is the origin of the Greater Israel plan, and why is there a concern over it?

Israel forging new alliances with key regional countries against Iran: NetanyahuIn an address on Tuesday, Netanyahu said Israel is forging ties with "important countries in the region" to confront what he called the "Iranian threat." Netanyahu's remarks point to a deepening alignment between Israel and parts of the Arab world, driven by shared concerns over Iran's military and nuclear ambitions.

How a British Walker on a Land Rover drew UAE from seven sheikhdomsThe UAE, which is under Iranian fire, existed as seven unmarked Trucial States till the 1970s. It was a British diplomat, Julian Walker, who drew the UAE's map by driving across the desert in his Land Rover and plotting tribal loyalties on paper.

Google now lets you change Gmail ID without losing mails, details hereGoogle has announced that you can now change your Gmail address. No longer do you need to carry an email address that you made years ago. Google CEO Sundar Pichai joked that while Gmail launched in 2004, your gmail address should not be stuck in time.

6 am email, 30,000 jobs gone: Oracle layoffs shock 12,000 in India, hit globallyMass layoffs at Oracle have reportedly impacted up to 30,000 employees globally, with around 12,000 job cuts in India alone, as workers describe sudden early morning termination emails and a lack of prior notice amid the company's AI-driven restructuring.

Live: Explosions heard in Tehran, Israel intercepts Yemeni ballistic missilesUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: As the Iran-US-Israel conflict intensifies, multiple flashpoints are emerging across the region, signalling a dangerous escalation. White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said President Donald Trump is open to asking Middle Eastern allies to help finance ongoing military operations against Iran, even as Tehran insists it will not step back without compensation. Meanwhile, thousands of US Army troops from the elite 82nd Airborne Division have begun arriving in the region, deepening Washington's military footprint.  Iranian sources also claimed a Mahan Air civilian plane bound for New Delhi on a humanitarian medicines mission was struck at Mashhad airport. In Tehran, Supreme Leader Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei, in a fourth written statement, hailed Revolutionary Guard Navy chief Alireza Tangsiri as a brave symbol of Iran's Gulf presence. He was killed last week in a strike. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

Who's losing the war in Iran?Iran doesn't have a lot to lose. The US has a lot: economy, petrodollar, hegemony, and face. Iran is not in a position to lose the war. It's winning. Simply by surviving, for another few weeks.

sports.headline: Shreyas Iyer in trouble? Yuzvedra Chahal updates on Punjab skipper's injury scare IPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Yuzvendra Chahal shared an update on Shreyas Iyer, who suffered an injury scare during Punjab Kings' clash against Gujarat Titans on Tuesday in Mullanpur.

Tiger Woods steps away from golf after DUI accident controversy, to seek help Tiger Woods has decided to step away from golf to focus on his health after pleading not guilty to DUI charges following a car crash in Florida. He has ruled himself out of the upcoming Augusta Masters to seek treatment.

Shreyas Iyer penalised in Punjab Kings' handsome IPL 2026 start IPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Punjab Kings captain Shreyas Iyer has been fined Rs 12 lakh for a slow over-rate offence during PBKS' first match against Gujarat Titans. Iyer scored 18 off 11 as Punjab beat Gujarat by three wickets in Mullanpur.

Candidates: Hyper-aggressive Pragg suffers major setback, slips to 3rd spot Candidates Tournament: Indian Grand Master R Praggnanandhaa slipped to third place at the FIDE Candidates tournament after a loss to Javokhir Sindarov. Pragg faltered while trying to be aggressive against the 20-year-old Uzbekistani.

End of Maxwell's Australia career? All-rounder snubbed from central contract Veteran all-rounder Glenn Maxwell has been omitted from Cricket Australia's central contract. This raises fresh questions over whether the 37-year-old still fits into the team's long-term plans in international cricket.

Inglis who? No BBL tax for Connolly as project Punjab finds a new No.3 general Cooper Connolly showed that he is one Big Bash League talent who can excel in the IPL, making the No.3 spot at PBKS his own after Josh Inglis' exit from the franchise. With lessons from Punjab legend Shaun Marsh, the all-rounder could prove to be a key player this season for the Kings.

IPL: Do Delhi Capitals have what it takes to end their title drought? IPL 2026, Delhi Capitals preview: Among the three teams still chasing a first IPL title, Delhi Capitals appear better equipped than ever, with a well-rounded squad that blends international experience and domestic depth. Yet, their familiar struggles with inconsistency and injuries could once again define how far they go.

Azzurri Apocalypse 3.0: Italy miss FIFA World Cup months after cricket joy In a surreal sporting paradox, Italy's recent ICC T20 World Cup heroics have been eclipsed by a footballing apocalypse: for the third consecutive time, the four-time world champions have failed to qualify for the FIFA World Cup, falling to a crushing penalty shootout defeat against Bosnia and Herzegovina in Zenica.

Living the IPL dream: Dushmantha Chameera aims to step up for Delhi CapitalsDushmantha Chameera calls IPL a dream and aims to step up for Delhi Capitals. The Sri Lankan pacer highlights mental strength as key to success in batting-friendly conditions ahead of the clash against LSG.

Shreyas Iyer cautious after injury scare in Mullanpur thriller: Don't want to jinx itShreyas Iyer remained cautious after a wrist injury during PBKS' win, saying he "doesn't want to jinx it". His knock was cut short, but debutant Cooper Connolly's 72 guided Punjab Kings to a tense victory over Gujarat Titans.

Kevin Pietersen praises Mumbai's 'outstanding' AQI, blasts paid promotion claimsFormer England captain Kevin Pietersen has sparked a social media debate after praising Mumbai's air quality as the "best" he has experienced, while also hitting back at users who accused him of doing paid promotion.

Cooper Connolly impresses on debut as PBKS outsmart GT in nervy winIPL 2026, PBKS vs GT: Australia's Cooper Connolly marked his IPL debut with a decisive 44-ball 72 as PBKS held their nerve to seal a three-wicket win over Gujarat Titans in IPL 2026's first thriller.

Williams scores on debut as India beat Hong Kong in Asian Cup 2027 QualifiersRyan Williams scored on debut as India beat Hong Kong 2-1 in Kochi to finish their AFC Asian Cup qualifying campaign with a win. Akash Mishra also got on the scoresheet as India ended their campaign with five points.

Tottenham appoint Roberto De Zerbi in desperate bid to stave off relegationTottenham Hotspur have turned to Roberto De Zerbi as a last-ditch attempt to salvage their Premier League status, appointing the Italian as their third manager of a turbulent campaign.

Watch: PSL ground staff nearly swept away after heavy wind in LahorePSL 2026: Ground staff were nearly swept away after strong winds lashed Lahore ahead of the clash between Islamabad United and Peshawar Zalmi at Gaddafi Stadium, with heavy rain forcing a delay in the toss.

Napoli-Romelu Lukaku feud: Italian giants planning to freeze out Belgian strikerNapoli confirm Romelu Lukaku missed training and face disciplinary action. Club considers suspension, contract implications as striker cites injury concerns.

Days after IPL withdrawal, Ben Duckett fined for speeding in EnglandDays after pulling out of the Indian Premier League, England Test opener Ben Duckett has been fined £700 after being caught speeding at 93mph while on his way to a game in his home country.

Gukesh opts for workload management, to skip part of Grand Chess tourD Gukesh adjusts his tournament schedule to focus on training and form recovery and has decided to skip part of the Grand Chess tour. Gukesh said he will only participate in two Rapid and Blitz tournaments in Europe.

Will Pant bat at No.3? LSG coach keeps everyone guessing ahead of DC clashJustin Langer keeps Rishabh Pant's batting position under wraps ahead of their IPL 2026 opening match against Delhi Capitals. Pant was rotated around the batting order during the IPL 2025 season, which didn't work out for him.

Who is Ashok Sharma? Uncapped pacer removes Jansen on IPL debut for TitansUncapped fast bowler Ashok Sharma, 23, made his IPL debut for Gujarat Titans in their IPL 2026 opener against Punjab Kings in Mullanpur on Tuesday after being bought for Rs 90 lakh at the Abu Dhabi auction, and struck a crucial blow by dismissing Marco Jansen for his maiden wicket to keep GT alive in a gripping contest.

Wouldn't have sacked Enzo Maresca as Chelsea coach in January: Marc CucurellaChelsea defender Marc Cucurella has claimed that he wouldn't have sacked Enzo Maresca mid-season and slammed the club's transfer policy of stacking up on young talents instead of going for established ones.

Fakhar Zaman handed 2-match suspension as ball-tampering row rocks PSLFakhar Zaman has been suspended for two PSL 2026 matches after being found guilty of ball-tampering during Lahore Qalandars' clash against Karachi Kings, with the PCB ruling that he breached the code for altering the condition of the ball.

RCB calmer than last year, players clear about their roles: Krunal PandyaRCB all-rounder Krunal Pandya believes the atmosphere in the Royal Challengers Bengaluru camp is much calmer than last year, with players having a clearer understanding of their roles this season.

Amelia Kerr continues fairytale run, becomes No.1 Women's T20I all-rounderAmelia Kerr and Georgia Voll scaled new heights in the latest ICC Women's T20I rankings, with the New Zealander becoming the No. 1 all-rounder and the Australian rising to the top spot among batters.

Indian prodigy Atiqa Mir ends WSK run as top female with P8 finishAtiqa Mir wrapped up her WSK Super Masters campaign with a strong P8 finish in a highly competitive field. The 11-year-old also emerged as the top-ranked female driver, underlining her rapid rise in international karting.

Five Red Lights: Suzuka shockwaves raise big questions over F1's new eraSuzuka exposed early cracks in Formula 1's new era, from safety concerns to shifting team dynamics. With tensions rising and a break ahead, the season already feels at a defining crossroads.

Test cricket important for other venues too: Ganguly reacts to Kolkata snubSourav Ganguly backed the expansion of Test cricket to newer venues despite Kolkata missing out on marquee fixtures. He emphasised the need to balance tradition with the broader growth of the format in India.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
